next_steps: Brain and leptomeningeal biopsy of affected areas
Immunohistochemical staining of biopsy tissue (MITF, HMB-45, Melan-A, S100)
Repeat MRI of the head with contrast including high-resolution black-blood sequences
Whole-body PET/CT scan to screen for occult primary melanoma

differential_diagnoses: Primary angiitis of the central nervous system
next_steps: Right frontal or parietal leptomeningeal and brain biopsy with tissue for histopathology and flow cytometry
Immunohistochemical staining of biopsy tissue for S100, HMB-45, Melan-A, and MITF
Molecular genetic analysis for GNAQ/GNA11 mutations and BRAF V600E mutation
Full-body skin examination and ophthalmologic slit-lamp examination to rule out occult primary melanoma
Whole-body PET/CT or BRAF-targeted imaging to identify potential occult primary systemic disease
Review of original angiography and embolization imaging to re-evaluate the primary 'fistula' diagnosis
